sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion are struggling, winless in their last 6 matches with 3 draws and 3 losses, conceding early in 4 of their last 5 games. Burnley are in worse shape, winless in their last 10 matches, with only 2 draws and conceding first in 7 of their last 8 games. Both teams are desperate for points, but Brighton’s home advantage could be key.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Recent head-to-head matches between Brighton and Burnley have been tight, with the last two encounters ending in 1-1 draws. Burnley have not lost to Brighton in their last 3 meetings, and both teams have scored in 4 of their last 5 matchups, suggesting a competitive game with goals on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brighton, sitting 14th with 25 points, have scored 28 goals (8th in the league) but conceded 27, showing defensive vulnerabilities. Burnley, 19th with just 12 points, have managed 20 goals but conceded 37, one of the worst defensive records. Brighton’s superior possession (52%) and pressing stats give them an edge over Burnley’s struggling attack and defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Brighton’s Danny Welbeck (8 goals, 4th in league) will be crucial upfront, while missing players like Yankuba Minteh could impact their width. Burnley miss key attacker Zian Flemming (5 goals), weakening their already poor offense. Brighton’s depth and home form might tilt the balance.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (53% home win, 23% draw, 24% away win) and Brighton’s better overall stats, they are favorites to win at the American Express Stadium. Burnley’s poor form and defensive issues make an away victory unlikely, though their head-to-head resilience could lead to a draw. Expect a closely fought match with Brighton slightly more likely to edge it.
